topic: autocss.com unreachable
symptom: Browser: ERR_SSL_VERSION_OR_CIPHER_MISMATCH / 'unsupported protocol'. Predates any code change.
diagnosis: DNSSEC chain broken. Two validating resolvers agree: Google 'No DNSKEY matches DS RRs' (EDE9), Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 'DNSSEC Bogus invalid SEP' (EDE6). With cd=1: NS=corey/leanna.ns.cloudflare.com, A=172.67.164.81/104.21.74.232 (Cloudflare proxied), DS@registry=tag2371 alg13 digest2 FAA0..CB75, zone DNSKEY KSK 257/3/13 mdsswUyr... DS at registrar does NOT match Cloudflare's active KSK -> SERVFAIL for all validating resolvers -> domain does not resolve -> SSL text is a downstream symptom.
cause: Stale/mismatched DS at the registrar (DNSSEC enabled then DNS moved to Cloudflare / key rotated without updating DS).
fix: User action only (external service; do NOT work around in code). Option A: Cloudflare dash > DNS > Settings > Disable DNSSEC, then remove DS at registrar; wait DS TTL (21600s). Option B: copy Cloudflare's current DS and replace the registrar DS so they match. After resolving, if SSL still errors, set Cloudflare SSL/TLS mode=Full and verify Universal SSL active.
tooling: No connected tool can fix this: Cloudflare MCP here is Developer-Platform-only (no DNS/DNSSEC/SSL tools); GoDaddy MCP only does availability/suggestions. Not grantable in-session.
rule: For unreachable custom domains, check DNSSEC (DS vs DNSKEY) FIRST via DoH with cd=1 before assuming an SSL/cert problem. CNAME file (GitHub Pages custom domain) is independent of index.html and DNS; editing index.html never affects it.

topic: autocss.com 503 after DNSSEC fix
symptom: After DNSSEC fixed (AD:true, DS now 2371/13/2 E51A8F3A.. matches Cloudflare KSK), browser TLS to edge succeeds (HTTP/2) but returns 503. Body: 'upstream connect error ... TLS_error SSLV3_ALERT_HANDSHAKE_FAILURE / HANDSHAKE_FAILURE_ON_CLIENT_HELLO'.
diagnosis: Second-layer issue: Cloudflare edge cannot complete TLS to the GitHub Pages origin. Verified origin healthy: autocss-com.github.io responds (server: GitHub.com), and forcing curl --resolve autocss.com:443:185.199.108.153 returns a VALID cert for autocss.com. So GH Pages serves the custom-domain cert correctly when reached directly; only the Cloudflare proxy->origin hop fails.
cause: Cloudflare proxy (orange-cloud) fronting GitHub Pages; proxy->origin TLS handshake mismatch. GH Pages + Cloudflare proxy is a fragile combo (proxy interferes with GH cert provisioning/renewal).
fix: User action (external service; no code workaround). PRIMARY: Cloudflare DNS -> set apex autocss.com + www to 'DNS only' (grey cloud) so browser hits GH Pages directly (proven working). ALT (keep proxy): SSL/TLS mode = Full (strict) and underlying record = CNAME to autocss-com.github.io (or A to 185.199.108-111.153).
rule: For GitHub Pages on a custom domain, prefer Cloudflare DNS-only (grey cloud); GH Pages provisions+serves its own HTTPS. Reserve the proxy only with Full(strict) + correct origin records. Diagnose proxy->origin 503s by reading the Cloudflare error body and testing the origin directly with curl --resolve.

topic: autocss.com 503 persists after DNS-only
symptom: User set apex to DNS-only. apex autocss.com now resolves to GitHub Pages 185.199.108-111.153 (www still proxied 172.67/104.21). Still HTTP 503; body now 'CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED: verify cert failed: verify SAN list'.
diagnosis: GitHub Pages has NOT provisioned the HTTPS cert for the custom domain autocss.com (it presents a cert whose SAN lacks autocss.com). Could never provision while the domain was proxied behind Cloudflare (ACME challenge blocked). Now DNS-only, GitHub can issue it but hasn't yet. NOTE earlier --resolve 'OK' test only proved TLS completed (curl exit 0 incl. 503), not 200 - it did not prove a provisioned custom-domain cert.
cause: Custom-domain cert not yet issued by GitHub Pages; needs DNS-only (done) + Pages settings re-trigger + time.
fix: GitHub repo Autocss-com/autocss > Settings > Pages: confirm custom domain autocss.com (DNS check should pass now), if stuck remove+re-add the domain to retrigger provisioning, wait for cert, enable Enforce HTTPS. Allow minutes-to-~1hr. Optionally grey-cloud www too.
tooling: Cannot verify a clean 200 from this container: outbound HTTPS goes through Anthropic egress proxy (Envoy) with strict SAN verification that fails while GitHub's cert is pending. A real browser is the truer test (but will also fail until the cert issues).
rule: GitHub Pages custom-domain HTTPS requires direct (DNS-only) resolution to provision its Let's Encrypt cert; a long-proxied domain must be un-proxied AND re-triggered in Pages settings. 503 'verify SAN list' = origin cert lacks the hostname = cert not provisioned.

topic: autocss.com RESOLVED - live over HTTPS
msg: Fully fixed. GitHub Pages settings showed custom domain autocss.com 'DNS check successful' and Enforce HTTPS greyed ('certificate has not yet been issued'). Shortly after DNS went direct, GitHub auto-provisioned the Let's Encrypt cert; now https://autocss.com = HTTP 200 (stable x3), served cert subject CN=autocss.com (verified even through the Anthropic egress proxy). Remove/re-add nudge was NOT needed in the end - DNS-only + a few minutes sufficed. Remaining nicety: user enables 'Enforce HTTPS' once the checkbox un-greys.
resolution_chain: 1) DNSSEC DS fixed at registrar -> resolves+validates. 2) Cloudflare apex set DNS-only (grey cloud) -> browser hits GitHub Pages directly. 3) GitHub Pages auto-issued custom-domain cert once unproxied -> HTTPS 200.

topic: initial-load trigger = CHECK the box (CORRECTION)
decision: The initial/return data call is triggered by CHECKING the nav radio (input.checked=true), NOT by .click(), NOT by dispatchEvent. Checking is the single signal that drives BOTH the CSS state machine (:checked visibility/active state) AND the data call - one source of truth ('preserves everything'). Programmatically setting .checked does NOT fire oninput, so the runtime runs the oninput lifecycle for the checked input directly. Return visits: read persisted endpoint from storage -> check THAT radio -> right data call + restored state. Without a checked box + loaded data, the data-driven CSS keeps everything hidden (otherwise nothing shows).
changes: (1) CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions line rewritten (was '...via programmatic nav radio .click()'). (2) oninput.js triggerInitialSelection: replaced target.click() with target.checked=true + runOnInputLifecycle(target.value); selection priority persisted -> statically-checked default -> first radio.
why: User correction: 'We are triggering the data call by checking the box! This preserves everything.' .click() simulates a user event (forbidden rules 11/12); dhcp app.js actually reads :checked and calls loadPageContent directly (no click) - we route that through oninput. topic: CORRECTION: nav default = NONE checked; selection is storage-driven
msg: User requirement: the HTML must have NO nav radio checked by default; on load, read browser storage for the end-user's last-session endpoint and CHECK that radio (which kicks off the oninput lifecycle = how initial load enters the lifecycle). VERIFIED THE REFERENCE: dhcp uses NO browser storage at all - its nav radios are JS-generated with the FIRST one checked (i===0 && index===0), and app.js initApp loads whatever is :checked, so dhcp DEFAULTS TO THE FIRST PAGE every visit (no last-selection restore). The storage-restore is the STARTER architecture convention (starter CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions: 'return visits read the persisted selection from storage and check that radio'); autocss oninput.js ALREADY implemented it (triggerInitialSelection: persisted-from-storage -> any-author-checked -> first radio; persistSelection writes each load; getInitialSelection reads it). The ONLY deviation was index.html hardcoding 'checked' on the manage radio. FIX APPLIED: removed that checked attribute (HTML default = none selected) + updated the oninput.js selection-priority comment. BROWSER-VERIFIED (chromium http, no console errors): static index.html has 0 checked nav radios; FRESH visit (empty storage) -> first radio 'manage' selected, h1 'Manage'; RETURN visit (localStorage autocss.app.v1 navigation.endpoint='audit') -> reload restores 'audit', h1 'Audit'. First-ever-visit fallback = first nav radio (matches dhcp default-to-first). Corrected SESSION-HANDOFF.md GROUND-TRUTH initial-load bullet. NOTE: this is a code change to the step-6 scaffold (index.html + comment) made at user instruction; commit separately.

topic: GENERAL PRINCIPLE: persist EVERY state-machine input's boolean state via oninput
decision: Per user: saving the selection to memory is NOT unique to the nav radios - it is true for ALL state-machine checkbox/radio inputs that run through the oninput lifecycle. Every such input persists its boolean state to browser storage (storage.js), and the runtime restores/can-manipulate those states later ('when we need them'). The nav persistSelection (oninput.js) is just the FIRST instance of this ONE general pattern; it applies equally to all durable state machines: which row is selected (list-item/row-toggle), which <details>/panels/sections are open, the header theme + layout toggles, etc. Net: the persisted store is a single source of truth for boolean UI state that is BOTH CSS-driven (:checked) AND restorable/controllable. NOT a new pattern - it generalizes the existing storage.js + oninput persistence the nav already uses. CURRENT: only nav selection persists today; generalizing (a storage-backed map keyed by each input's name/role, written on its oninput, re-applied on load by checking the inputs so each enters its own oninput lifecycle) is a step-7+ task. SCOPE NUANCE (-> OPEN QUESTION 8, confirm before building): momentary ACTION triggers that check->act->uncheck (Save/Reset/Delete/Close/New) have no meaningful resting state (always unchecked); the meaningful persisted states are the DURABLE toggles/selections; also settle the storage key/shape and the load-time restore order so re-applying booleans does not double-fire the data lifecycle. Recorded as decision (D) + Open Question 8 in SESSION-HANDOFF.md.
why: User architectural directive: 'This allows these boolean states to be controlled and manipulated when we will need them later.' Captured as a first-class principle; implementation deferred to the step-7+ rebuild with the scope confirmed first.

topic: CLARIFICATION: no orchestrated load/restore order; oninput is ONLY triggered by the state machines
msg: User correction to my 'restore order on load' framing (Open Question 8c). The model: oninput is NEVER an automatic standalone lifecycle - it is ALWAYS triggered by a radio/checkbox state-machine input. The ONLY automated startup actions are (1) read browser storage and (2) check the right nav radio. There is NO orchestrated restore order and NO separate 'fire the lifecycle' step. Every other input's oninput fires when the USER selects it. Relevant HTML is display:none by default and only appears (display:grid) when it receives content via :has()/:not(:empty), so the load order is natural and one-at-a-time. MECHANISM NOTE (agreed, already how the code works + CLAUDE.md documents it): programmatically setting input.checked=true does NOT dispatch oninput in the browser, so for the single automated nav selection the runtime runs THAT radio's same oninput handler once (the exact handler a user click would run - not a separate path). For generalized persistence/restore: re-checking a persisted input restores a CSS-VISUAL toggle for FREE (CSS reacts to :checked, no oninput/data needed); only the nav selection is data-bearing at startup. ACTION: settled Open Question 8(c) (no orchestrated order); refined decision (D) wording in SESSION-HANDOFF.md; (a) which-inputs + (b) storage-shape remain to confirm before building. No code change.

topic: dispatchEvent IS NOW ALLOWED — single sanctioned exception for the programmatic initial-load nav trigger
decision: User ruling: use dispatchEvent for the initial/restore nav selection. The startup script SELECTS the nav radio (storage else first), sets .checked=true, then input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})) so the radio's OWN oninput fires the lifecycle - the script NEVER calls the lifecycle directly. RATIONALE (user's criteria, all satisfied): reusable one-liner; cross-browser (Event ctor + dispatchEvent are universal); framework-INTEROPERABLE (dispatching a real input event is the standard programmatic-trigger path that testing libs/integrations use) - only known quirk is React's controlled-component value-tracker, IRRELEVANT here (zero-dependency vanilla); intuitive to devs; AVOIDS click/onclick (which user wants discouraged). SCOPE: dispatchEvent is the SINGLE SANCTIONED exception - used ONLY for the programmatic selection on load/restore (the ONE place with no real user event). ALL real user interactions fire oninput natively (no dispatch); e.g. the step-7 row-toggle's handleRowToggle runs from the checkbox's OWN real oninput and drives the row-select with a DIRECT call (no dispatch). REVERSES the prior blanket ban: CLAUDE.md JS Runtime Conventions said 'never a synthetic .click() or dispatched event'; PROGRESS.json meta.rules + SESSION-HANDOFF + earlier records listed dispatchEvent as FORBIDDEN - ALL UPDATED. Also flips VERIFIED-DHCP-FACT #5: dhcp's radio.dispatchEvent is no longer 'forbidden->replace with direct call'; it's the same idea, now allowed (row-toggle still uses a direct call since it already has a real event). NOTE: ORIGINAL-PROMPT.md (verbatim historical) still bans dispatchEvent; this decision AMENDS that blanket ban - do NOT edit the historical prompt. CODE CHANGE APPLIED: oninput.js triggerInitialSelection now dispatches input instead of calling runOnInputLifecycle directly; comment updated. BROWSER-VERIFIED (chromium http, no console errors): fresh visit -> manage -> h1 'Manage' (data loaded via the dispatched event, no direct call); storage seeded audit -> reload -> h1 'Audit'.
why: User chose dispatchEvent as the standards-native, interoperable, click-free way to programmatically enter the oninput lifecycle; confirmed not limiting in any way the user cares about.

topic: CORRECTION to decision (D): SAVE all state-machine inputs, but auto-RESTORE is NAV-ONLY (not yet for others)
msg: User refines (D). (1) State always changes through USER input, with the sole exception (so far) of first load (the sanctioned dispatchEvent nav selection); oninput is independent and decoupled from other processes. (2) INTENT still stands: every state-machine input (radio/checkbox) SAVES its boolean state to storage on its own oninput, so those booleans can be controlled/manipulated programmatically 'when we need them later.' (3) BUT do NOT auto-RESTORE them on load - NOT YET. The ONLY state restored from browser history on load is the NAV radio input. So the earlier (D) wording 'the runtime restores those states on return' + 'RESTORED on load by re-checking the persisted inputs' was WRONG -> corrected: SAVE = future step-7+ task (durable inputs); RESTORE = nav-only now, auto-restore of other inputs stays OFF until the user enables it. Open Question 8 updated: restore-scope DECIDED (nav-only); open parts = (a) which inputs save + action-trigger handling, (b) storage key/shape; (c) settled (no orchestrated order; oninput decoupled). Updated SESSION-HANDOFF.md decision (D) + Open Question 8. No code change (only nav save+restore is wired today; nothing else persists yet).

topic: SAVE/DELETE DATA-MODEL DEFECT — full root-cause analysis + fix plan
msg: INVESTIGATION (why Save records land in the wrong place) — READ IN FULL before attempting the fix. === SYMPTOM === Clicking Save in the <aside> form appears to do nothing in the UI: the new/edited record never shows up in the data table, yet the network write SUCCEEDS (HTTP 200). The created records pile up invisibly, and the as-is Delete cannot remove them. === EVIDENCE (live mockapi, 2026-06-10) === Each endpoint is a mockapi COLLECTION (an ARRAY of top-level resources). The app only ever uses element [0], whose .items[] are the rows shown. But Save POSTs created NEW top-level resources (siblings of [0]), not entries in [0].items. Cross-endpoint top-level counts found: manage=28(!), faqs=8, option-set=4, audit=2, servers=2, api-registration/option-types/scope-type/server-types/credentials/variables/settings=1. The extras are write-to-wrong-level residue (Save POSTs) plus some mockapi auto-seed. The stray records have the shape {id, name, avatar, createdAt, itemName, itemType, ...} — i.e. ITEM-LEVEL field names stranded at the RESOURCE level, decorated by mockapi's default faker fields (name/avatar/createdAt are the manage resource's configured faker schema, NOT app data). === EXACT CODE PATH (autocss, as-is port of dhcp) === READ side (oninput.js:96-98): const raw = Array.isArray(data) ? data[0] : data; const record = normalizeRecord(endpoint, raw); record.items = normalizeItems(endpoint, raw.items||[]); -> the visible rows are raw.items of the FIRST collection element. WRITE side (forms.js Save handler, ~lines 193-217): submitCheckbox.onchange => { const selected = querySelector('ul li input[name="list-item"]:checked'); const id = selected?.closest('li')?.querySelector('label > id')?.textContent?.trim(); const endpoint = querySelector('nav input[name="nav"]:checked')?.value; const data={}; fieldset non-readonly fields -> data; const payload = denormalizeRecord(endpoint, data); if(id) putJson(`${endpoint}/${id}`,payload) else postJson(endpoint,payload); }. api.js resolveEndpoint:33-40 turns 'manage' into POST {API_BASE}/manage. === ROOT CAUSE 1 (FUNDAMENTAL: read/write model mismatch) === The READ model is NESTED (collection[0].items[n]) but the WRITE model is FLAT (POST to the collection root creates collection[n]). mockapi semantics: POST /manage creates a new element in the manage COLLECTION; it does NOT and cannot append to an existing resource's items[] array. There is NO code path anywhere that writes into record[0].items, and the flat mockapi resource exposes NO nested route (e.g. /manage/:recordId/items). So even a 'correct' POST physically cannot land where the rows are read from. This is the core defect; everything else compounds it. === ROOT CAUSE 2 (id-lookup bug = VERIFIED DHCP FACT #2) === forms.js:202 does querySelector('label > id') looking for an <id> element, but the row's id cell is <id-> (toTagName appends a trailing hyphen to single-word keys: id -> id-, in inject.js toTagName). <id> NEVER matches -> id is ALWAYS undefined -> the if(id){putJson} branch is DEAD -> Save ALWAYS takes else{postJson} (create), never updates. The Delete handler has the SAME bug (forms.js:258 querySelector('label > id')) -> id undefined -> Delete takes the no-id branch (DOM remove only) and NEVER calls deleteJson, so it can't remove server records either. === ROOT CAUSE 3 (why strays look like half-rows) === denormalizeRecord('manage',{name,type}) maps to {itemName,itemType} (schema.js ENDPOINT_SCHEMAS), so the created resource carries ITEM field names at the RESOURCE level. mockapi then auto-adds the resource's faker fields (name, avatar, createdAt). Net stray shape: {id,name,avatar,createdAt,itemName,itemType}. === WHY NOTHING IS 'FAILING' === The API writes succeed; this is an ARCHITECTURE problem, inherited verbatim from the dhcp prototype where mockapi (a convenience/faker backend) was used as if each endpoint were a single nested document, while it is actually a flat collection of resources the app reads only [0] of. === FIX DIRECTION (future data/compliance session; pick ONE consistent model) === OPTION A (least churn, keep current API): (1) fix the id selectors forms.js:202 and :258 from 'label > id' to 'label > id-' (matching the real cell tag) so id resolves. (2) Re-model the WRITE so it operates on record[0]: on Save, read the current record[0] (the one with title+items), splice the edited item into record[0].items by its id (or append for new), then PUT {endpoint}/{record0.id} with the whole updated resource; on Delete, remove the item from record[0].items and PUT the resource. (3) This requires the read layer to retain record[0]'s real top-level id (currently oninput.js takes data[0] but the rendered record drops/ignores the resource id; must carry it through, e.g. keep raw[0].id available to forms.js, likely via a small shared accessor in oninput.js, NOT global state). OPTION B (cleaner data model, more work): restructure the backend so each ITEM is its own mockapi resource with real per-item routes (POST/PUT/DELETE {endpoint}/{itemId}) and point BOTH read and write at the same level; the app would fetch a flat list per endpoint instead of [0].items. Either option MUST: stay D7460N (oninput-only triggers, no data-*, CSS state, querySelector, idempotent/stateless, one API base in api.js); keep normalize/denormalize symmetric; preserve the read rendering; and be verified via the REAL trigger in chromium-over-http with a throwaway record that is then cleaned up. === SCOPE: AFFECTS ALL 12 ENDPOINTS === Every endpoint uses the [0].items read model, so the same Save/Delete defect applies to all of them, and several already carry residue at the top level (faqs +7, option-set +3, audit +1, servers +1, manage +11 seed-ish after cleanup). The fix is endpoint-agnostic (it lives in forms.js + the read accessor), so fixing it once fixes all. === CLEANUP STATUS === Deleted ONLY manage's 16 clearly-Save-shaped strays (had itemName/itemType, no items/title) via direct API DELETE (200 each); manage 28->12. LEFT untouched: manage's 11 seed-shaped records ({name,avatar,createdAt} only) AND all residue on other endpoints (faqs/option-set/audit/servers) because (a) the user's instruction targeted the Save strays and (b) distinguishing mockapi SEED from Save RESIDUE is unreliable automatically (the faker 'name' field collides with the real 'name' data field). A future cleanup should audit every endpoint and review borderline records MANUALLY before deleting. === RELATION TO LEDGER === This is the downstream consequence of the id-lookup ledger item (FACT #2). Elevated to its own first-class ledger entry: 'SAVE/DELETE DATA-MODEL DEFECT'. Do NOT fix during the parity build; this is a dedicated future session.

topic: MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM — color-scheme question RESOLVED to system `light dark`; color-scheme.css build prompt drafted + committed
msg: RESOLUTION (user-confirmed 2026-06-14): the long-open open_q 'color-scheme dark (DHCP) vs Rule 19 light dark' is RESOLVED in favor of Rule 19 — :root{color-scheme: light dark} (system/OS-follow as the DEFAULT, NOT forced dark). PLAN: the color/theme concern is being rebuilt as a MODULAR, drop-in (copy/paste-able) system in PARTS, each its OWN future session + handoff. Part 1 = color-scheme.css (THIS draft): the light/dark GOVERNOR + system-preference adaptation + a semantic fg/bg variable contract, independent of any palette. Part 2 = color-theme-*.css: the PALETTE, ultimately derived from a SINGLE hex in the FILE NAME (e.g. color-theme-default.css, color-theme-339933.css) via hue/alpha (+ future filter/transform 'glass'/'isometric'). Part 3 = HTML controls (the scheme control + a hue picker that reads hues from linked color-theme-*.css filenames). SPLIT: today's assets/css/themes.css splits — light/dark MECHANISM + the toggle override move to color-scheme.css (REMOVE color-scheme:dark and :root:has(header label:last-of-type input:checked){color-scheme:light} from themes.css); the 24-token OKLCH palette becomes color-theme-default.css (Part 2). SCHEME CONTROL DECISION: replace the single header ☼ checkbox (header label:last-of-type, index.html:39-42) with a THREE-STATE radio group Light/Dark/System (state-machine radios in <label>s, one name group), System radio `checked` by default. Each radio maps to an ABSOLUTE color-scheme via pure CSS :has(): :root{color-scheme:light dark} (System default follows OS); :root:has(header input[name="scheme"][value="light"]:checked){color-scheme:light}; :root:has(...[value="dark"]:checked){color-scheme:dark}. System checked -> no override rule -> stays light dark. `value` is a standard attr (already used by nav radios), not data-*. This three-state model removes the 'permanent override' trap of a boolean toggle and needs NO matchMedia/compare logic. JS DECISION (the ONE place Part 1 touches JS): selection persistence + on-load restore wired INTO the EXISTING init path (initializeOnInputLifecycle()/triggerInitialSelection() in oninput.js) — do NOT create a new init function. Reuse storage.js read/writePersistent on the SAME state object (key autocss.app.v1) with a new `colorScheme` key beside `navigation`, MIRRORING the existing getInitialSelection()/persistSelection() pair. ON LOAD: if absent or 'system' -> DO NOTHING (System radio stays checked, CSS follows OS); if 'light'/'dark' -> set input.checked=true on the matching radio (radios auto-uncheck the rest). NO dispatchEvent here (:has(:checked) reacts to the property directly; dispatch stays reserved for the nav-radio DATA call). ON SELECT: bind the scheme radios' oninput (like bindNavOnInput, input.oninput=..., the sanctioned property — Rule 12: NO addEventListener) to persist the selected value ('light'/'dark'/'system'). JS ONLY ever sets the HTML control's .checked — NEVER writes styles; CSS does the styling. VARIABLE CONTRACT (semantic, veteran+noob-friendly, Tailwind/Bootstrap-mappable): foreground --fg(->CanvasText), --fg-link(->LinkText), --fg-link-hover(->LinkText/ActiveText), --fg-accent(->AccentColor), --fg-emphasis(=--fg-accent), --fg-disabled(->GrayText), --fg-muted(->GrayText); mirrored --bg(->Canvas), --bg-link, --bg-link-hover, --bg-accent(->AccentColor/Highlight), --bg-emphasis(=--bg-accent), --bg-disabled(->ButtonFace/Field), --bg-muted(->ButtonFace). Each defaults to a CSS SYSTEM COLOR so a theme-less drop-in still looks right (and forced-colors/contrast work for free); color-theme files (Part 2) REASSIGN the SAME names via light-dark(); use @layer so the theme layer wins. DETECT ALL FOUR media prefs: prefers-color-scheme, forced-colors:active, prefers-contrast(more|less), prefers-reduced-transparency:reduce. FOUC: keep <meta name=color-scheme> in <head> and align its content to 'light dark' (currently 'dark light' at index.html:11); research the UA-stylesheet / early-color-scheme interaction (declaring color-scheme early lets the browser paint the correct Canvas before author CSS loads) — you cannot edit the UA stylesheet, so MIRROR its system-color conventions. DELIVERABLE THIS SESSION: COLOR-SCHEME-BUILD-PROMPT.md (repo root) committed on claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2 = the ready-to-paste Part-1 build prompt. It is a PRELIMINARY DRAFT (subject to refinement before the build session), not the final spec. STATUS: PROMPT + DOCS ONLY — NO app code changed. The Part-1 BUILD (create color-scheme.css, swap the header checkbox for the 3-radio group in index.html, wire the oninput persistence/restore in oninput.js, split themes.css, align the meta, add the <link>) is the NEXT session. Open items to settle early are listed at the bottom of COLOR-SCHEME-BUILD-PROMPT.md (token system-color choices; --txt/--bg/--accent -> --fg/--bg bridge + whether themes.css stays until Part 2; 'don't create a new function' scope; exact radio markup/aria).

topic: color-scheme.css prompt — focus indicator moved to the LABEL + made a transformable layer (not outline)
msg: User correction to the focus-ring design in COLOR-SCHEME-BUILD-PROMPT.md: (1) the state-machine <input>s must stay VISUALLY HIDDEN but remain KEYBOARD-FOCUSABLE (no display:none/visibility:hidden); the focus indicator appears on the associated <label>, NOT the input — drive it with `label:has(> input:focus-visible)`. (2) The indicator must NOT be a CSS `outline` (outline can't be cornered, animated, or transformed). It must be a STYLABLE, TRANSFORMABLE layer on the label (pseudo-element painted from --outline) because future sessions will: shape it into 'tech-corner' brackets, blink it in via intro animation, transition it from one focused label to the next as the user tabs, and transform it with isometric 3D + other effects. (3) forced-colors EXCEPTION: decorative pseudo-element/box-shadow is stripped under forced-colors, so a REAL `outline` fallback (system Highlight/CanvasText) is required in @media (forced-colors: active) — the one place outline is correct. (4) Part-1 scope is just the --outline COLOR (accent-hued, AccentColor default, Part-2 derives from the single theme hex) + the label:has(:focus-visible) hook + the forced-colors outline fallback; the tech-corners/intro-blink/tab-transition/isometric-transform styling is a FUTURE session (Part 3/effects) and Part 1 must not block it. Updated the focus section, the foundation-application bullet, and the verification recipe. Prompt-only; committed on claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2.

topic: CORRECTION (MDN-verified): CSS outline capabilities + <label> focus — focus-indicator primitive left as decide-at-build + Part-2 research task
msg: Two MDN-verified corrections to the focus-indicator design in COLOR-SCHEME-BUILD-PROMPT.md. (1) OUTLINE: my earlier claim that a CSS `outline` 'cannot be cornered, animated, or transformed' was WRONG. Per MDN /Web/CSS/outline: outline DOES follow `border-radius` (rounded corners), supports `outline-offset`, has animatable `outline-color`/`outline-width`/`outline-style`, and transforms WITH its element (it's outside the box model for LAYOUT only). What outline genuinely canNOT do: render as DISCONTINUOUS 'tech-corner' brackets, be transformed/animated INDEPENDENTLY of its element, or be a SINGLE indicator that travels label->label. (2) LABEL FOCUS: per MDN /Web/HTML/Element/label, a <label> is NOT in the tab order; implicit association (input nested in label) needs no id/for, but KEYBOARD FOCUS LANDS ON THE INPUT, not the label. Clicking a label only FORWARDS focus to the control (pointer behavior). So the verified-correct mechanism is: keep the state-machine input visually hidden but FOCUSABLE (never display:none/visibility:hidden) and paint the indicator on the LABEL via `label:has(> input:focus-visible)`. Making the label itself focusable would need tabindex + JS activation and would break the :checked/radio single-source-of-truth. USER DECISION (2026-06-14, AskUserQuestion): DOCUMENT BOTH indicator primitives and decide at build time, PLUS add a research task to investigate new HTML/CSS capabilities in Part 2 before choosing. Prompt now: corrects the false outline claim; documents OPTION A (standalone transformable layer: pseudo-element or anchor-positioned element — needed for tech-corner brackets, independent isometric-3D transform, traveling indicator; needs a forced-colors `outline` fallback) vs OPTION B (CSS outline on the label — simplest, survives forced-colors natively, but continuous/per-element only); keeps the --outline accent-hued color token + the label:has(:focus-visible) hook as Part-1 scope; and adds a Part-2/effects RESEARCH TASK (anchor positioning, @property, clip-path/border-image/conic-gradients, @view-transition/scroll-driven animation) to finalize A-vs-B with evidence. Prompt-only; committed on claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2.

topic: MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM Part 1 — JS selection persistence + on-load restore wired into existing oninput.js init/storage
msg: Round 3 of Part 1: wired color-scheme selection persistence + on-load restore into the EXISTING oninput.js init/storage plumbing (no new system, no new init function), mirroring the nav pair. ADDED to oninput.js: (1) bindSchemeOnInput() [mirrors bindNavOnInput] — binds each `input[type=radio][name=scheme]`.oninput to persistColorScheme(input.value); pure-CSS color so NO runOnInputLifecycle call and NO dispatchEvent (dispatch stays reserved for the nav data call); (2) restoreColorScheme() [mirrors triggerInitialSelection] — reads getInitialColorScheme(); if 'light'/'dark' sets that radio.checked=true (radios auto-uncheck the group) with NO dispatchEvent (`:has(:checked)` reacts to the property directly); if absent or 'system' does NOTHING (System radio stays HTML-checked, CSS follows OS); (3) getInitialColorScheme() [mirrors getInitialSelection] — reads state.colorScheme from readPersistent('autocss.app.v1','autocss.app.v1',{}); (4) persistColorScheme(value) [mirrors persistSelection] — merges {...current, colorScheme, updatedAt} via writePersistent (localStorage-primary, cookie-fallback) so colorScheme sits BESIDE navigation/environment in the one state object. WIRED into initializeOnInputLifecycle() after bindNavOnInput()/triggerInitialSelection(): bindSchemeOnInput(); restoreColorScheme(). JS sets ONLY radio.checked (the HTML control), never styles — CSS reacts to :checked. Idempotent/stateless; matches the file's comment/naming style; node --check (ESM) passes. Selectors are name-scoped (input[name=scheme]) = DOM-agnostic, consistent with color-scheme.css. STILL TO DO in Part 1: the --txt/--bg/--accent->--fg/--bg consumer bridge; the focus-indicator paint; BROWSER VERIFICATION (standalone CodePen test + chromium: pick Light/Dark/System -> reload -> chosen radio restored from storage; stored light/dark stays fixed when the emulated OS pref changes; System/none follows OS; assert JS sets only checked never inline styles; Light<->Dark<->System round-trips with no permanent override; + media-emulation for all 4 prefs + no FOUC). NOT yet browser-verified. Committed + pushed to claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2.

topic: MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM Part 1 — FIX: color-scheme persistence was gated behind data hydration (user-reported: choice not saving cross-session)
msg: USER REPORT (real device; autocss.com via GitHub Pages serving the branch): picked Light on a dark-OS phone; a new tab did NOT restore Light -> the choice never saved. ROOT CAUSE (reproduced AND fixed in a REAL browser — Playwright + chromium headless, colorScheme:dark emulation, local static serve of the exact branch HEAD 866dc2a): bindSchemeOnInput() + restoreColorScheme() were called AFTER `await hydrateShell()` inside initializeOnInputLifecycle(); when hydrateShell() throws/stalls (API unreachable/slow/cert/timeout — requestData returns null on a failed fetch, then injectNavText(null) throws), the entire post-await block is skipped, so the scheme radios are NEVER bound to persistColorScheme and restore never runs. The CSS color flip still works (pure :has(:checked), zero JS) -> it LOOKS like it works but never persists = exactly the reported symptom. REPRO PROOF: API failing -> clicking Light flips computed color-scheme to 'light' but localStorage colorScheme stays null; API succeeding -> all 5 checks pass (Light saves+restores, System round-trips to OS-follow, no permanent override). FIX (oninput.js): moved bindSchemeOnInput() + restoreColorScheme() to run FIRST, synchronously, BEFORE `await hydrateShell()`. Color is pure UI and must not depend on the data API; the scheme radios are static markup present at parse time, so up-front bind/restore is safe (also cuts FOUC since restore no longer waits on the network). NOT a workaround for an external-service failure — it decouples a UI toggle from data transport, which the architecture already mandates (CSS owns color). RE-VERIFIED WITH API DOWN: clicking Light now persists (storage 'light') and restores on reload despite hydrateShell throwing; System round-trip + OS-follow all pass. Nav binding stays gated behind hydrateShell (unchanged; nav needs the data) = separate concern, untouched. Pages note: autocss.com 503'd from the CI egress during diagnosis (could not fetch the live file from here), so diagnosis ran against a local serve of branch HEAD. Committed + pushed to claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2.

topic: Simplicity mandate + future interoperability/CDN goal + on-load restore consolidation + color-scheme rename + hydrateShell questioned
msg: USER DIRECTION (2026-06-15): (1) PRINCIPLE elevated — the app's whole priority is to do what everyone over-engineers, but SIMPLE/direct/declarative-first/browser-first/non-complex => easy/fast/small/cheap/interoperable; LESS complexity NEVER more; every change must REDUCE complexity. (2) FUTURE GOAL (post-MVP, track): reference instances embedding this ONE UI into each major CMS + framework to demonstrate interoperability, all served from ONE CDN instance. (3) NAMING: use 'color-scheme' NOT 'scheme' everywhere (HTML name=, CSS selectors, JS, storage key) for intuitive/consistent developer naming. (4) ARCHITECTURE intent: ONE on-page-load function that does one-to-many things; two of them are select-nav-radio-from-storage (first if none) and select-color-scheme-radio-from-storage — same pattern for both. (5) hydrateShell() questioned as unnecessary complexity. CLARIFICATION OF THE TANGLE (for future me): three SEPARATE concerns were conflated — (a) ON-LOAD RESTORE = read storage, flip nav + color-scheme controls; (b) PAGE-DATA CRUD = oninput fetches the SELECTED page's data, needed, stays; (c) hydrateShell() = a pre-fetch of nav LABEL text + banner text injected into otherwise-blank nav/banner. Restore (a) does NOT need hydrateShell (c); they're independent. hydrateShell is load-bearing ONLY for nav label text + banner text (nav VALUES manage/faqs/etc. are ALREADY static in index.html; version is a constant). OPEN DECISION put to user via AskUserQuestion: make nav labels + banner STATIC HTML so hydrateShell can be DELETED entirely (most declarative/CDN-friendly, simplest) vs keep nav labels data-driven. Awaiting answer before cutting — data-driven nav was a prior build step (#6 generation/inject), so reversing it needs explicit user confirmation per heuristic step-2 + 'confirm before dropping'. NEXT once decided: rename scheme->color-scheme, consolidate the on-load restore into one clearly-named function, and remove/keep hydrateShell accordingly.

topic: Simplify per user: delete hydrateShell() + isShellHydrated session flag; rename scheme->color-scheme; ONE on-load function (Least Power, no session tracking)
msg: Per user (LESS complexity, Least Power, no session tracking): (1) DELETED hydrateShell() wrapper + the isShellHydrated module flag + the 'Shell hydrated' log. The banner/nav-label/version injection is now inlined as simple sequential calls inside the SINGLE on-page-load function initializeOnInputLifecycle(), which runs ONCE from app.js — no once-per-session guard (nothing re-calls it; per-page data goes through runOnInputLifecycle via the nav radio's own oninput, NOT through this). (2) KEPT unchanged: injecting nav LABEL text from the API into the existing static HTML nav items (injectNavText), banner text (injectBanner), version (injectVersion) — 'just the text content into the existing html', exactly as the code already did. (3) RENAMED scheme -> color-scheme everywhere: index.html name=color-scheme (x3); color-scheme.css selectors :root:has(input[name=color-scheme][value=light|dark]:checked) + label:has(>input[name=color-scheme]:checked) + comments; oninput.js bindSchemeOnInput -> bindColorSchemeOnInput (+ its selector) and restoreColorScheme's querySelector. Storage key stays camelCase `colorScheme` (consistent with sibling navigation/environment/updatedAt). On-load order: color-scheme bind+restore FIRST (pure CSS, no data, can't be skipped by a data hiccup), THEN inject API text (banner/nav/version), THEN nav bind + triggerInitialSelection (selecting the nav radio fires its OWN oninput = the only data call). BROWSER-VERIFIED (Playwright/chromium headless, OS=dark): API-DOWN -> color-scheme still persists/restores/round-trips (decoupled from data); API-UP -> nav labels inject (Manage / Frequently Asked Questions / API Registration / Audit), nav radio 'manage' checked, article H1 'Manage', 10 rows (data path intact) + color-scheme 5/5. node --check passes. FUTURE NOTE (user, cross-session): later the nav menu items THEMSELVES will be BUILT programmatically from JSON key/value pairs (like table cells via toTagName), replacing the static nav skeleton; for now only text is inserted into existing nav items. Build prompt COLOR-SCHEME-BUILD-PROMPT.md left as historical spec; final naming color-scheme supersedes its 'scheme' examples.

It also owns the light/dark mechanism (:root{color-scheme:light dark} + 3-state Light/Dark/System :has() override). PHASE 2 = color-theme-??????.css where ?????? = the 6-hex of the base color (hex in the FILENAME only, for brevity); contents are ONLY variable OVERRIDES written in OKLCH so the whole palette derives from that ONE base color; it overrides via the NATURAL CSS CASCADE — just linked AFTER color-scheme.css in <head>, no @layer needed, no cross-file reference (CSS superpower: later file wins by load order). topic: all loaded CSS files wrapped in @layer <filename>; fixes the @layer-layout spacing regression (claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2)
msg: User: 'make sure all CSS files have @layer [nameof file].' Wrapping layout.css alone (prior commit) had put it in a layer BELOW the still-UNLAYERED reset.css, whose `*{margin:0;padding:0}` then beat every layout spacing rule (layer-origin trumps specificity) -> app went flush to the viewport edges (user screenshot). Layer order now follows index.html LINK order (first-seen): reset < fonts < color-scheme < color-theme < layout < inputs < media < typography < scrolling < a11y < forms < fallbacks < loading. reset is therefore LOWEST -> its margin:0 loses to layout -> outer spacing restored. No master @layer order statement added (link order suffices; no more/less). Relative priority among the formerly-unlayered files is unchanged (they kept link order) so no new regressions expected; only reset dropped below layout (the intended fix). Minimal wrappers (no re-indent). radii.css NOT wrapped (not linked in index.html). node 7/7. User will visually verify.

msg: Per user: every CSS file gets its OWN named layer so the ABSENCE of a wrapper is never mistaken for intent. Wrapped the two remaining unlayered loaded-or-reference files: radii_.css -> @layer radii, themes.css -> @layer themes (both inactive/not linked; harmless). transitions.css already has an INTERNAL @layer view-transitions but NO file-level wrapper; left it UN-file-wrapped for now and flagged to user because it is commented-out + slated for full rebuild AND contains @view-transition (whose behavior inside a cascade layer is uncertain) — user to decide. DOCUMENTED the principle in CLAUDE.md Rule 29 (the canonical doc = 'global+contextual notes'): every file wrapped in @layer <filename>; NO master order statement; index.html <link> order IS the layer/cascade order (the C in CSS, used deliberately); reset loads first = lowest; PURPOSE = pre-emptive deconfliction so any UNLAYERED style a future dev/framework writes wins by default with no specificity fight. This is also why wrapping layout.css alone broke spacing earlier (reset was still unlayered = above layout) and why uniform layering fixed it. node 7/7.

topic: Hosting model answered: static front-end on GitHub Pages; dynamic layer = external backend (claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2)
msg: User asked: can each demo be hosted+run entirely from its own repo on GitHub Pages? ANSWER recorded: front-end YES (D7460N is 100% static -> Pages serves the UI directly, custom domains e.g. international.dance, no build). Dynamic layer NO from Pages alone: GitHub Pages is STATIC-ONLY (no server, no DB, no SECRETS, repo is public) so login/secure-area, saved favorites+notes, and payments+store CANNOT live in the repo/on Pages. Those run against an EXTERNAL hosted backend (auth + DB + payment processor) the static SPA reaches via the existing CRUD JS transport (oninput -> api.js). This is NOT a compromise of D7460N -- it IS the architecture (JS = transport to external APIs; the 'backend' is never the repo or Pages). Payment secret keys especially must never sit in a public repo -> checkout via the processor's hosted flow or a thin backend.
